qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation in PUT handler enables denial of service via OOM kill
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the HTTP PUT upload handler's unbounded io.ReadAll(req.Body) memory allocation and resulting availability loss. The advisory targets the SFTP subsystem's sanitizePath() raw prefix check, which lets authenticated SFTP users read and write sibling filesystem paths outside the configured root.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SFTP root escape in goshs. The only final qualifying finding concerns HTTP PUT request-body memory exhaustion, not the SFTP sanitizePath() prefix-validation flaw or its file-operation sinks.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5h6h-7rc9-3824 describes an authenticated SFTP jail escape caused by sanitizePath() in sftpserver/helper.go using strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, sftpRoot) without a directory-boundary check.
The session input manifest lists only one final qualifying finding: unbounded_put_memory_exhaustion.

unbounded_put_memory_exhaustion/vulnerability_report.json identifies httpserver/updown.go:39, where the HTTP PUT handler reads req.Body with io.ReadAll without a size limit, causing denial of service by memory exhaustion.
The finding does not identify the advisory's vulnerable SFTP functions, sanitizePath() prefix check, sibling-prefix bypass, or unauthorized outside-root file read/write behavior.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the target advisory root cause, so advisory-tier runtime testing was not applicable.
